result:
All gates green. Final summary follows.

ROUND 11 (Session 11) COMPLETE — sealed implementer report, graph-clean record-cell wrap model.

RESULTS (acceptance gates)
- 80 tests green (73 prior + 7 new probe-derived fixtures); GRAPHCLEAN_CORPUS roundtrip 12022/12022; alloc_corpus 12022/12022.
- fill_census before -> after: ALL cells 96.580% -> 98.794% (608422/615850); wrapping 86.26% -> 95.60% (136262/142540); single-cell wrapping 97.41% -> 100.00% (11419/11419); multi-cell wrapping 85.29% -> 95.21%; false-flat 819 -> 324; false-wrap 1478 -> 1150; fill misses 18764 -> 5954.
- Probe-battery byte-exactness (every battery ever captured): 822/862 wrap cells (95.4%): G 84/86, H 55/58, I 99/101, K 63/70, A 43/44, B 19/20, C 33/34, D 11/12, E 66/68, F 50/51, p2 133/133, p3 81/89, p4 7/10, r8 78/86.
- Absent-override behavior byte-identical to group_widths, regression-gated (supplied_cell_widths_override_estimates, supplied_trigger_width_overrides_self_width, raw_rule_supplied_widths_reach_cells).

FAMILY OUTCOMES
- FAMILY 1 (fill allocation): five new laws, all live-probe-pinned (batteries G/H/I/K, 189 rules): (a) fill rounding is round-HALF-DOWN (equal pairs 43/43); (b) occupancy is RECURSIVE: elems+1 per tuple/union node, elems-1 when directly nested in a tuple, full inside func args (K1 flip at 38, K2 at 39); (c) fill numerator INCLUDES tuple surcharges capped at 7 per node (K3: 6-tuple receiver ribbon 38; r8 16/20-elem grids force the cap; Wide fixture still byte-exact); (d) the self-budget bonus is gated on the LAST argument being the tuple/union (WIT battery); (e) the tuple opener HANGS (`<` left at line end, elements below at the fill column, also inside func args; fact/func openers never hang) — closed nearly the whole band-NONE family (corpus rows 6612 -> 854) and drove single-cell wraps to 100%.
- FAMILY 3 (false wraps): a RELIEF second trigger pass — a pass-1-wrapping cell renders flat iff flat <= max(87 - sum(charges), 20), truly-broken wrapping siblings (fill < flat-2) charging their fill allocation, peel-only/flat siblings their C, no bonus (batteries I/IB/TB4; corpus false-wraps 1478 -> 1150).
- FAMILIES 2 and 4 DISSOLVED by direct probe (battery J, with ?unabbreviate= twins): the reference lays out POST-abbreviation display text — abbreviated cells with internal widths 96-150 render flat, and sibling budgets follow display occupancy. The round-9/10 "wrap decided on un-abbreviated width" belief is REFUTED. The family-4 corpus witness (St_I(..., SI4)) is really a 2-cell row [St_I 77, Fr 9] wrapping by )-peel at its bonus-free budget. I did NOT build the requested internal-text/substitution cell-document — it would model behavior the reference demonstrably lacks; instead CellWidths.trigger_width (self-width override, both trigger passes, incl. lone cells vs 87) was added and test-gated. INTERFACE.md now documents that the display-text estimates ARE the probed reference behavior.

HONEST RESIDUE: the +-1 coupled-fits relief boundary (IA_65_23; [45,43] saved vs [46,42] not — no closed form separates them), large-tuple numerator approximation (cap 7; r8 grids 78/86), ~850 band-NONE corpus rows (deep func-chain/abbreviation-expansion layouts, not an internal-width effect).

FILES: crate /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/graphdot/workspace/graph-clean (src/generate.rs two-pass law + recursive CellShape + trigger_width; src/doclayout.rs tuple hang; src/bin/band_dump.rs 20-field dumps; tests/generate_tests.rs round-11 fixtures). Evidence: workspace/QUERIES.log Session 11, workspace/BEHAVIOR.md (Section 3f Session-11 bullet, Section 8, round-11 report), workspace/INTERFACE.md (rewritten), workspace/r11/ (probe theories, captures, band TSVs, fitting scripts). Servers: probeG-K ran on ports 3200-3204, all stopped; ports 3200-3299 verified clear. No forbidden paths read; no commits made.
